name: Hyperspace Beacon
description: Hyperspace beacon, fixed navigation platform, immobile and built to tell lost ships where not to die. For captains who think star charts are for cowards.

name: Advanced V-19 Torrent Starfighter
description: Slayn & Korpil V-19 Torrent, single-seat starfighter with Republic service history and a need for speed. For buyers who want to feel like a being in a coffin with engines.

name: Corellian YT-1300 Transport
description: Corellian Engineering Corporation YT-1300, light freighter, crew two with room for six and a serious cargo hold. Built like a steakhouse and handles like a bistro.

name: AEG-77 Vigo Transport
description: AEG-77 Vigo, armed transport, eight crew and six passengers with enough guns to discourage enthusiasm. Perfect for convoy escort, discreet freight work, and very public denials.

name: Corellian YT-1930 Transport
description: Corellian Engineering Corporation YT-1930, transport, crew two and six passengers with bigger cargo ambitions than its older cousin. For operators who liked the classic model but wanted more hold and less romance.

name: TT-6 Landspeeder
description: SoroSuub TT-6, two-seat landspeeder, cheap, reliable, and easy to fix with whatever is in the garage.

name: Hardcell-Class Interstellar Transport
description: Techno Union Hardcell-class, heavy interstellar transport with troop room, cargo volume, and all the grace of a flying tax office.

name: CloakShape Fighter
description: CloakShape fighter, one-seat starfighter with laser cannons and concussion missiles. Please sign this waiver before test flying.

name: The Visionary
description: Eleaor Propulsion Starscape-class yacht, cloaked luxury transport with a droid crew and privateer attitude. Don't like paying crew wages huh?

name: LAVr QH-7 Chariot
description: Uulshos Manufacturing LAVr QH-7 Chariot, command speeder, three crew, armored enough for officers who prefer danger at a respectful distance.

name: OG-9 Homing Spider Droid
description: Baktoid Armor Workshop OG-9, heavy walker-droid with long legs, big guns, and no opinions worth hearing.

name: RC-2 Twilight Scoutship
description: Incom RC-2 Twilight scoutship, one pilot plus two extra seats, long-ranged and built for stealthy recon. Are you a spook?

name: Corellian YT-2000 Transport
description: Corellian Engineering Corporation YT-2000, light transport with room for crew, passengers, and a respectable amount of cargo. Too expensive for nerfherders, too cheap for royalty.

name: Venator-Class Star Destroyer
description: Kuat Drive Yards Venator-class, carrier-destroyer built to launch fighters and end arguments at fleet scale. Spacious, authoritative, and only slightly overqualified for most neighborhood disputes.

name: N-1 Royal Starfighter
description: Theed Palace Space Vessel Engineering Corps N-1, single-seat royal starfighter with astromech socket and more polish than shame. Please, don't put your dirty hands on the chrome.

name: BARC Speeder
description: Aratech BARC speeder, one rider plus one passenger, built for recon, speed, and regrettable confidence. The preferred choice for couriers who think brakes are a personal insult.

name: Strike-Class Medium Cruiser
description: Strike-class medium cruiser, compact warship with enough guns to seem polite right up until it isn't.

name: MC80 Star Cruiser
description: Mon Calamari MC80 star cruiser, deep-space capital ship with heavy shields and flagship energy. For trap sensing admirals.

name: Delta-7 Aethersprite Interceptor
description: Kuat Systems Engineering Delta-7 Aethersprite, Jedi interceptor, one pilot plus astromech and very little spare room for comfort. Built for those annoying cheddar monks.

name: Corellian Barloz-Class Medium Freighter
description: Corellian Engineering Corporation Barloz-class, medium freighter, crew two with room for four and plenty of cargo. Workmanlike, dependable, and very ugly.

name: Y164 Slave Transport
description: Y164 slave transport, prison-hauler with a big crew and a worse reputation. I wouldn't fly one of these in polite society.

name: The Virago
description: MandalMotors StarViper prototype, single-seat assault craft with teeth, speed, and no interest in blending in.

name: I4 Ionizer Starfighter
description: I4 Ionizer, single-seat starfighter with astromech support and a clear interest in ruining somebody else's electronics.

name: SkyBlind Recon Ship
description: SkyBlind recon ship, light scout transport with room for a team and enough hold space to justify the paperwork.

name: The Bloody Credit
description: Gallofree Yards GR-75 medium transport, eighteen crew and a cargo profile that has seen some ugly manifests. As-is sale, and no, no shipfax.

name: Maka-Eekai L4000 Transport
description: Maka-Eekai L4000, mid-size transport with room for crew, passengers, and serious freight volume. A practical long-hauler for buyers who think glamour is what delays delivery.

name: F9-TZ Transport
description: F9-TZ transport, heavy repulsor hauler with forty-passenger capacity and all the charm of a loading dock. Slow, sturdy, and perfect for moving troops who have already accepted disappointment.

name: Aerosled Mark III
description: Aerosled Mark III, one-seat speeder built for simple travel and uncomplicated excuses. Reliable local transport for someone who swears they only need it for errands.

name: PL-90 Luxury Speeder
description: PL-90 luxury speeder, chauffeur-friendly repulsor limo with seating for seven and no tolerance for dust. For buyers who want traffic to feel like a class system.

name: Imperial RaDAir
description: Imperial RaDAir, compact Imperial airspeeder with official lines and unofficial uses. Fleet gray, tidy profile, and guaranteed to make every checkpoint feel personal.

name: Y-Wing "LongProbe"
description: Koensayr Y-wing LongProbe, reconnaissance refit with extended sensors and the same old workhorse attitude. For crews who like their starfighters ugly, durable, and still somehow employed.

name: Corellian YZ-2500 Heavy Transport
description: Corellian Engineering Corporation YZ-2500, heavy transport with commercial bones and enough space to make accountants smile. A serious freighter for serious operators and slightly unserious cargo declarations.

name: Punworcca 116-Class Interstellar Sloop
description: Punworcca 116-class interstellar sloop, fast small craft with two crew and room for eleven. A genteel little star-yacht for people who like their travel elegant and faintly suspicious.

name: Baas-Class Space Station
description: Baas-class space station, large orbital installation with a heavy crew requirement and all the mobility of a building. Excellent parking, terrible getaway options.

name: CR70 Corellian Corvette
description: Corellian Engineering Corporation CR70, early corvette with a diplomatic silhouette and plenty of conversion potential. For buyers who want one hull that can do escort duty, relief work, or mildly deniable rebellion.

name: Gymsnor-3 Light Freighter
description: Gymsnor-3 light freighter, small cargo ship with four passenger seats and enough hold for real work. A no-nonsense hauler for captains who consider style to be wasted mass.

name: TIE Defender
description: Sienar Fleet Systems TIE Defender, single-seat high-end starfighter with shields, hyperdrive, and Imperial overcompensation in physical form. For the pilot who looked at a regular TIE and said, 'Yes, but more.'

name: LAAT/i Gunship
description: Rothana Heavy Engineering LAAT/i, assault gunship, six crew and thirty troops with very direct ideas about insertion. If diplomacy fails, this is what low-altitude diplomacy looks like.

name: AT-KT
description: AT-KT walker, two-crew custom walker with the usual armored stomp-and-glare package. A battlefield mascot if your idea of a mascot can step on a bunker.

name: Corellian VCX-350 Light Freighter
description: Corellian Engineering Corporation VCX-350, light freighter, crew three with room for eight and a real cargo appetite. Ideal for merchants, smugglers, and merchants who resent the distinction.

name: The Crimson Axe
description: Crimson Axe, pirate frigate with hangars, prisoners, and exactly the kind of silhouette insurance adjusters hate. A roaming headquarters for anyone who thinks boarding actions are a personality trait.

name: S-130 Shelter Medical Speeder
description: S-130 Shelter, medical speeder built for casualty pickup and urgent exits. Clean panels, soft ride, and only slightly haunted.

name: Mobile Spacedock 220
description: Mobile Spacedock 220, slow support platform built to repair ships rather than impress anyone. The galactic equivalent of bringing a garage a war zone.

name: Cutlass-9 Patrol Fighter
description: Cutlass-9 patrol fighter, single-seat interceptor with just enough cargo room to pretend it has hobbies. A trim little customs headache for anyone on the wrong side of an inspection.

name: Mu-2 Long Range Shuttle
description: Mu-2 long-range shuttle, utility transport built to go farther than its pilot wants to talk about. The sort of ship sold as 'versatile' right before you discover what it was versatile at.

name: Archaic Dreadnaught-Class Heavy Cruiser
description: Rendili StarDrive Dreadnaught-class, old heavy cruiser with enormous crew requirements and an even older reputation. Still intimidating, if you can afford to staff every corridor and every grudge.

name: Cardan III-Class Space Station
description: Cardan III-class space station, armed orbital platform with batteries, torpedoes, and nowhere urgent to be. A fixed address for anyone who wants their hospitality backed by turbolasers.

name: J-1 Shuttle
description: J-1 shuttle, two-crew transport with seating for twenty and enough hold space to matter. A practical box with engines for buyers who judge ships by whether they arrive.

name: 74-M Speeder Bike
description: 74-M speeder bike, one rider plus one passenger, all speed and very little patience. Perfect for scouts, couriers, and anyone who thinks the safe route is for other people.

name: A-A5 Speeder Truck
description: A-A5 speeder truck, heavy civilian hauler, three crew and room for twenty-five plus cargo worth declaring creatively. Rugged, roomy, and ideal for entrepreneurs with a flexible relationship to customs law.

name: Flare-S Swoop
description: Mobquet Flare-S, sport swoop, one rider and one passenger with a habit of reaching 650 km/h before your judgment catches up. Clean frame, responsive throttle, and ideal for buyers who hear 'reckless' as a compliment.

name: Imperious-Class Star Destroyer
description: Imperious-class Star Destroyer, capital warship, crewed heavy and built to carry troops, guns, and administrative overreach. A fine flagship for anyone who likes their diplomacy visible from orbit.

name: VT-49 Decimator
description: Kuat Drive Yards VT-49 Decimator, assault ship, crew two to four with six passenger seats and eighty tons of cargo. Imperial styling, generous firepower, and enough interior space for all your worst intentions.

name: Sentinel-Class Landing Craft
description: Sienar and Cygnus Sentinel-class, assault shuttle, crew six with room for fifty-four troops and a 1,000 km/h approach speed. Perfect for rapid deployment, stern arrivals, and making a landing zone feel judged.

name: Corellian Consular-Class Cruiser
description: Corellian Engineering Consular-class cruiser, sleek diplomatic hull with more speed than armor and better manners than most corvettes. A tasteful little starship for clients who prefer their trouble wrapped in ceremony.

name: A-7 Hunter Interceptor
description: Kuat Drive Yards A-7 Hunter, one-seat interceptor with no hyperdrive, no shields, and no patience. For pilots who looked at a TIE and thought, 'Almost, but let me pay extra for better taste.'

name: AT-AP
description: Rothana AT-AP, three-crew artillery walker, sixty kilometers per hour and enough guns to overcorrect a battlefield. Stable platform, long reach, and only mildly embarrassing to park.

name: Pelagia Duplex Command Assault Gunship
description: Pelagia Duplex command assault gunship, compact military flyer with gunship teeth and command deck attitude. For officers who want to supervise the fight while also being the loudest part of it.

name: Hound's Tooth
description: Corellian YV-666, heavy freighter, single pilot with prisoner space, twenty tons of cargo, and the subtle charm of a bounty notice. Rugged, roomy, and ideal if your passenger list is mostly unwilling.

name: SPHA-M
description: SPHA-M, self-propelled heavy artillery, siege platform built to solve distant problems very permanently. Factory-fresh intimidation for buyers who think range is a moral virtue.

name: Air-2 Racing Swoop
description: Air-2 racing swoop, one rider and one passenger, all engine with just enough chassis to make it legal somewhere. Fast, twitchy, and sold only to people who sign waivers without reading them.

name: The Last Resort
description: The Last Resort, armed light transport, crew two with ten passenger seats and forty tons of hold space. Name aside, it runs fine, and desperation is just another form of customer loyalty.

name: Corellian CR-20 Troop Transport
description: Corellian Engineering CR-20, troop transport, crew six with forty soldiers and fifteen tons of cargo. A dependable box of engines and doctrine, now available without the usual paperwork.

name: Charger c70 Corellian Consular-Class Cruiser
description: Corellian Engineering Charger c70, consular cruiser turned warship with a cleaner silhouette than its intentions. Ideal for customers who like their republics armed and their escorts deniable.

name: Scimitar Assault Bomber
description: Sienar Scimitar, two-crew assault bomber, shielded, fast, and built to carry a very convincing amount of explosives. For refined buyers who want their bombing runs to feel almost upscale.

name: TIE Bomber
description: Sienar TIE Bomber, one-seat strike craft with a bomb bay, no shield bubble worth bragging about, and all the personality of an invoice. Honest Imperial hardware for when precision has already failed.

name: Manta Droid Subfighter
description: Manta droid subfighter, unmanned aquatic attack craft built for underwater pursuit and deeply unpleasant surprises. Low crew costs, low conversation, and excellent commitment to the bit.

name: Prototype Dreadnaught-Class Heavy Cruiser
description: Rendili prototype Dreadnaught-class, heavy cruiser with the usual oversized hull and prototype-level confidence. A collector's piece for admirals who think teething problems count as character.

name: AEG-77 Vigo Gunship
description: AEG-77 Vigo, gunship variant, compact transport chassis upgraded with less patience and more weapons. Same reliable frame, now marketed to customers with stronger opinions.

name: M3-A Scyk Fighter
description: MandalMotors M3-A Scyk, light interceptor, one pilot, tight cockpit, and just enough ship to start a bad idea. Cheap, nimble, and beloved by everyone who considers fragility somebody else's problem.

name: The Grinning Liar
description: The Grinning Liar, armed freighter, crew two with six passenger seats and ninety tons of cargo. The name is branding, the hold is spacious, and nothing in the registry should concern you.

name: U-LAV
description: U-LAV, two-crew air vehicle, four hundred kilometers per hour and a profile that says 'military budget meeting.' Crisp handling for anyone who wants a gunship without the extra ceremony.

name: V-35 Courier
description: V-35 Courier, one pilot, two passengers, and just enough cargo room for a package worth lying about. Modest, practical, and perfect for errands that become stories later.

name: Munificent-Class Frigate
description: Hoersch-Kessel Munificent-class, frigate, crew-heavy with room for troops and the broadside to prove a point. A Banking Clan classic for clients who want compound interest with turbolasers.

name: Herald-Class Shuttle
description: Herald-class shuttle, crew two with five to ten passengers and enough cargo space to feel useful. Smart little courier for buyers who want their business to arrive before the rumors do.

name: TIE Shuttle
description: Sienar TIE Shuttle, one pilot, two passengers, and one ton of cargo squeezed into an Imperial compromise. All the charm of a TIE, now with just enough legroom to file a complaint.

name: Tri-Scythe-Class Frigate
description: Tri-Scythe-class frigate, sharp-nosed warship with troop capacity and a very committed silhouette. Ideal for customers who want their frigate to look like it already bit someone.

name: Nune-Class Imperial Shuttle
description: Nune-class Imperial shuttle, crew eight with thirty-five passengers and two hundred fifty tons of hold space. Plenty of room for personnel, materiel, and the sort of orders nobody enjoys.

name: The Ebon Hawk
description: Dynamic-class freighter, crew three with room for eight and forty tons of cargo under the floor and behind the legend. Well-traveled, heavily storied, and somehow still easier to buy than replicate.

name: MR/RV
description: MR/RV, reconnaissance and recovery vehicle, six crew, four passengers, two tons of kit, and all the warmth of military utility. A dependable crawler for buyers who enjoy terrain the hard way.

name: Toscan 8-Q Starfighter
description: Toscan 8-Q, two-seat starfighter with extra passenger room and more cargo than a ship this awkward deserves. Ugly, capable, and perfect for pilots who value function over every other human experience.

name: Corellian YV-545 Light Freighter
description: Corellian Engineering YV-545, light freighter, crew two with five passenger seats and eighty tons of cargo. Straightforward hull, healthy hold, and no pretensions beyond getting paid.

name: CK-6 Freeco Speeder Bike
description: CK-6 Freeco, one-seat speeder bike with cold-weather attitude and just enough cargo for a survival kit. Starts in ugly climates and looks proud of it.

name: The Banshee
description: The Banshee, fast transport, crew two with eight seats and thirty-five tons of cargo behind the bulkheads. Smooth ride, strong engines, and a name chosen so the approach is half the sale.

name: Nova-Class Battlecruiser Refit
description: Nova-class battlecruiser refit, old heavy bruiser brought up to newer standards and older grudges. Perfect for buyers who think modernization should still feel vindictive.

name: Droch-Class Boarding Ship
description: Droch-class boarding ship, compact assault craft with room for six droid troops and a deeply transactional worldview. A fine choice if knocking politely has never tested well.

name: Corellian Crix-Class Courier Shuttle
description: Corellian Crix-class courier shuttle, crew five with thirty passenger seats and fifty tons of cargo. Fast enough for urgent deliveries and respectable enough to lie about them.

name: CX-133 Chaos Fighter
description: CX-133 Chaos Fighter, one-seat starfighter, fast, compact, and exactly as trustworthy as the name suggests. A flashy little purchase for pilots who enjoy alarms as ambient sound.

name: AT-AA
description: AT-AA, anti-air walker, three crew, sixty kilometers per hour, and built entirely around ruining somebody else's flyby. Fresh from the factory and already looking upward with intent.

name: Miy'til Command Starfighter
description: MandalMotors Miy'til command starfighter, one pilot, command-grade systems, and enough edge to offend a standard cockpit. For commanders who prefer to lead from the sharp end.

name: Canderous-Class Assault Tank
description: Canderous-class assault tank, three-crew tracked brute, fifty kilometers per hour and built to settle arguments at street level. A tasteful amount of overkill for the discerning ground campaign.

name: H-60 Tempest Bomber
description: H-60 Tempest, four-crew bomber with a fast approach and the carrying capacity to make that everybody's problem. Elegant in the way only ordnance platforms think they are.

name: Protector VI Patrol Speeder
description: Protector VI, patrol speeder, one driver and six seats for passengers, deputies, or other complications. City-ready, official-looking, and excellent for pretending this stop is routine.

name: Tsmeu-6 Personal Wheel Bike
description: Tsmeu-6, personal wheel bike, one rider and one passenger with walking mode for when dignity must briefly return. Distinctive engineering for buyers tired of ordinary mistakes.

name: Helot-Class Medium Space Transport
description: Helot-class medium transport, workaday cargo hauler with the glamorous profile of a receipt. Reliable lift, respectable volume, and none of the charm that drives up the price.

name: Corellian Crix-Class Assault Shuttle
description: Corellian Crix-class assault shuttle, crew five with fifty passengers and fifty tons of cargo when the mission gets crowded. A courier line that clearly made some new friends in weapons procurement.

name: StarViper-Class Attack Platform
description: MandalMotors StarViper, one-seat attack platform with elegant lines and deeply unelegant intentions. Premium handling for customers who want menace to come with curves.

name: Star Saber XC-01 Starfighter
description: Star Saber XC-01, one-seat starfighter with interceptor speed and barely enough room for restraint. A sleek little rocket for pilots who think subtlety is dead weight.

name: AeroChaser Speeder Bike
description: AeroChaser, one-seat speeder bike, light, quick, and carrying exactly four kilograms of priorities. Great starter model for anyone eager to make local authorities familiar with their silhouette.

name: DP20 Corellian Frigate
description: Corellian Engineering DP20, fast frigate, ninety-one crew and enough guns to make escorts feel underdressed. Sharp profile, quick response, and ideal for owners who prefer their corvettes angry.

name: The Millennium Falcon
description: Corellian YT-1300, heavily modified light freighter, crew four and six passengers with a hold that has seen things. Fast, famous-looking, and previously loved by someone who treated maintenance like a philosophical concept.

name: Recusant-Class Light Destroyer
description: Recusant-class light destroyer, long-hulled warship, three hundred crew and forty passengers. Slim profile, heavy guns, and ideal for buyers who want a capital ship with a permanent sneer.

name: Ghtroc 720 Freighter
description: Ghtroc 720, light freighter, crew two and ten passengers with 135 tons of cargo space. Honest commercial lines for captains who want their smuggling to look aggressively middle-management.

name: WLO-5 Speeder Tank
description: Ubrikkian WLO-5, speeder tank, crew three and four passengers at 110 kilometers per hour. Rugged pirate chic, lightly used, and only fired in self-defense if you ask the seller.

name: Corellian YU-410 Light Freighter
description: Corellian Engineering YU-410, light freighter, crew four and twelve passengers with 250 tons of hold space. Roomy, respectable, and perfect for the buyer whose cargo manifests read like fiction.

name: Seltiss-2 Caravel Cabin
description: Seltiss-2 Caravel Cabin, small transport, one pilot and six passengers with five tons of cargo. Civilized little shuttle for clients who want their travel modest, tasteful, and plausibly billable.

name: Corellian YT-1250 Freighter
description: Corellian Engineering YT-1250, light freighter, crew two and five passengers with eighty tons of cargo. Strong bones, clean silhouette, and enough hold space to support several regrettable ventures.

name: TL-1800 Light Freighter
description: TL-1800, light freighter, crew two and seven passengers with 110 tons of cargo. Reliable independent-trader energy for buyers who like their ships practical and their paperwork imaginative.

name: Jumpstar HPF Starfighter
description: JumpStar HPF, single-seat starfighter, fast, compact, and carrying just seventy kilos of priorities. A tidy interceptor for pilots who want hyperdrive and poor impulse control in one package.

name: Eta-2 Actis Heavy-Defense Interceptor
description: Kuat Systems Engineering Eta-2 Actis, one-seat interceptor, tiny, sharp, and built to turn harder than your nerves can. Premium Jedi-adjacent styling for owners who enjoy flying something that looks annoyed.

name: Davaab-Type Starfighter
description: Davaab-type starfighter, single-seat interceptor with forty kilos of cargo and no spare patience. Fast little predator for customers who think comfort is just wasted mass.

name: Dreadnaught-Class Heavy Cruiser
description: Rendili Dreadnaught-class, heavy cruiser, big-gun bruiser with a crew bill that never quite stops hurting. A classic naval brick for admirals who mistake endurance for charm.

name: Lucrehulk-Class Battleship
description: Lucrehulk-class battleship, gigantic trade-ring warship, crewed by the hundreds and visibly compensating from orbit. Spacious, ominous, and excellent for buyers who prefer their commerce armed.

name: Victory II-Class Star Destroyer
description: Victory II-class Star Destroyer, wedge-shaped capital ship with enough mass to make diplomacy feel performative. Solid Imperial curb appeal for clients who want every arrival to count as a warning.

name: Scarab-Class Droid Starfighter
description: Scarab-class droid starfighter, unmanned attack craft, quick, disposable, and utterly free of cockpit complaints. Low overhead, high aggression, and ideal for buyers who dislike pilot unions.

name: Medlifter Troop Transport
description: Medlifter troop transport, crew two and four passengers with a ton of hold space for urgent problems. A practical little evac crate for anyone who calls chaos 'field conditions.'

name: GAT-12h Skipray Blastboat
description: Sienar GAT-12h Skipray, attack boat, one pilot, heavy weapons, and a very unhealthy attitude toward targets. A gentleman's gunship for buyers who want subtlety available only as an optional accessory.

name: TX-130T Fighter Tank
description: TX-130T, repulsor tank, crew two and five passengers at 193 kilometers per hour. Sleek armor for customers who want battlefield presence without giving up the joy of reckless cornering.

name: Talon I Combat Cloud Car
description: Talon I combat cloud car, compact aerial fighter, single pilot, fast climb, and no social grace whatsoever. A fine weatherproof purchase for patrol work and dramatic overreactions.

name: GPE-7300 Space Transport
description: GPE-7300, space transport, one pilot and four passengers with forty-five tons of cargo. Efficient, unglamorous, and perfect for buyers who treat starships like spreadsheets with engines.

name: The Glorious Chariot
description: Royal Naboo N-1 derivative, one-seat starfighter with polished lines and absolutely no humility. Tasteful finish, spirited performance, and previously owned by someone who enjoyed entrances.

name: T-65B X-Wing Starfighter
description: Incom T-65B, one pilot and astromech, long-range starfighter with 110 kilos of cargo and very strong opinions. The dependable classic for farmboys, idealists, and anyone else who keeps ruining empires.

name: T2-B Repulsor Tank
description: T2-B, repulsor tank, crew three, eighty kilometers per hour, and enough armor to make infantry feel judged. A sturdy field piece for buyers who like their support vehicles blunt and loyal.

name: SCT Scout Craft
description: SCT scout craft, crew two and eight passengers with 275 tons of cargo and a scout label that feels generous. Long-legged utility ship for operators whose recon somehow always requires freight capacity.

name: "Vulture" Droid Starfighter
description: Baktoid Vulture droid starfighter, unmanned interceptor, fast, ugly, and entirely committed to the cause of swarming. Economical fleet filler for buyers who value quantity over survival instincts.

name: Star Galleon-Class Frigate
description: Star Galleon-class frigate, bulky transport-escort, crew 150 with room for 300 troops and cargo that arrives under guard. A sensible purchase if your shipping philosophy begins with 'comply.'

name: Conqueror-Class Assault Ship
description: Conqueror-class assault ship, crew three with twenty-five tons of hold space and a thousand-kilometer pace. Compact, aggressive, and named with the sort of confidence that voids warranties.

name: NovaSword Space Superiority Fighter
description: NovaSword, single-seat superiority fighter with clean lines and a deeply competitive worldview. A polished duelist for pilots who want every dogfight to feel like a résumé.

name: AT-TE
description: All Terrain Tactical Enforcer, seven crew and thirty-eight passengers with ten tons of cargo and zero interest in subtle movement. Battlefield-proven walker for buyers who like their transport to also be an argument.

name: StarForge Station
description: StarForge station, fixed orbital industrial nightmare, one operator and ten passengers in theory, much more dread in practice. Rare legacy infrastructure for clients who prefer their factories spiritually hostile.

name: STAP
description: Baktoid STAP, one-rider hover scout, four hundred kilometers per hour and barely enough vehicle to count. Great fuel economy, excellent panic factor, and no room at all for second thoughts.

name: ILH-KK Citadel-Class Civilian Cruiser
description: ILH-KK Citadel-class, civilian cruiser with unusually stern lines and the confidence of a warship wearing a fake mustache. A tasteful option for buyers who want 'non-military' to sound negotiable.

name: T-65XJ3 X-Wing Starfighter
description: Incom T-65XJ3, one pilot and astromech, updated X-wing with better speed and less patience. Same iconic silhouette, now for customers who liked the classic but wanted the sequel budget.

name: Gladius-Class Light Freighter
description: Gladius-class light freighter, crew one and two passengers with fifty tons of cargo. Lean little courier-hauler for owners who want their shipping solution to look faintly threatening.

name: Luke's Snowspeeder
description: Incom T-47, two-seat airspeeder refit for cold weather. Don't pay any mind to the crushed skeleton in the back.

name: Ministry-Class Orbital Shuttle
description: Ministry-class orbital shuttle, crew two and thirty-six passengers at a respectable 750 kilometers per hour. Official-looking transport for clients who want their bureaucracy to arrive in person.

name: Swift Assault 5 Hovercraft
description: Swift Assault 5, hovercraft, crew four and six passengers at 200 kilometers per hour with a very literal product name. If you need a raid vehicle that sounds like a marketing meeting, this is the one.

name: Miy'til Starfighter
description: MandalMotors Miy'til, single-seat starfighter, sharp, compact, and designed like it resents compromise. A premium purchase for pilots who think armor and culture should both be visible.

name: Nova-Class Battlecruiser
description: Nova-class battlecruiser, heavy fleet ship with six hundred troops and six hundred tons of cargo because moderation failed. A proper statement piece for governments in a threatening mood.

name: Delta-7 Aethersprite High-Speed Interceptor
description: Kuat Systems Engineering Delta-7 Aethersprite, one-seat interceptor, tiny cockpit, sharp turns, and cheddar monk-grade visibility problems. Very responsive craft for buyers who mistake serenity for overconfidence.

name: R-41 Starchaser
description: Hoersch-Kessel R-41 Starchaser, single-seat starfighter with 110 kilos of cargo and enough speed to attract the wrong sort of attention. Budget menace for pilots who want performance and can live without dignity.

name: E-Wing Escort Starfighter
description: FreiTek E-wing, advanced escort starfighter, one pilot, modern systems, and the kind of confidence only New Republic procurement can buy. A sleek next-generation choice for customers who enjoy winning with paperwork.

name: Subjugator-Class Heavy Cruiser
description: Subjugator-class heavy cruiser, oversized warship, crew twenty-five with sixty passengers and a title that really tells on itself. Ideal for fleet buyers who find diplomacy too slow and subtle.

name: B-Wing Shuttle
description: B-wing shuttle conversion, one pilot and two passengers with just forty-five kilos of spare room. A practical refit for anyone who looked at a starfighter and said, 'Yes, but errands.'

name: X-83 TwinTail Starfighter
description: X-83 TwinTail, one pilot and astromech, high-speed starfighter pushing 1,200 kilometers per hour. Sharp-nosed and expensive-looking, perfect for buyers who want their heroics factory-installed.

name: Ton-Falk-Class Escort Carrier
description: Ton-Falk-class escort carrier, fleet support hull with troops, hangars, and the social warmth of a docking invoice. A sensible acquisition if you need a carrier that looks like it means overtime.

name: Modified ILH-KK Citadel-Class Civilian Cruiser
description: Modified ILH-KK Citadel-class, civilian cruiser refit by someone who clearly did not trust the word civilian. Same elegant shell, now with the kind of upgrades sellers describe as 'tasteful improvements.'

name: Junker TYE-Wing
